describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text.quote:
Neurodegenerative diseases pose a complex ﬁeld with various neuronal subtypes and distinct differentially
                                affected intra-neuronal compartments. Modelling of neurodegeneration requires faithful in vitro separation
                                of axons and dendrites, their distal and proximal compartments as well as organelle tracking with
                                deﬁned retrograde versus anterograde directionality. We use microﬂuidic chambers to achieve
                                compartmentalization and established high throughput live organelle imaging at standardized distal
                                and proximal axonal readout sites in iPSC-derived spinal motor neuron cultures from human amyotrophic
                                lateral sclerosis patients to study trafﬁcking phenotypes of potential disease relevance. Our semi-
                                automated pipeline of organelle tracking with FIJI and KNIME yields quantitative, multiparametric high
                                content phenotypic signatures of organelle morphology and their trafﬁcking in axons. We provide here the
                                resultant large datasets to enable systemic signature interrogations for comprehensive and predictive
                                disease modelling, mechanistic dissection and secondary hit validation (e.g. drug screens, genetic screens).
describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text.note: Extracted text evidence: Neurodegenerative diseases pose a complex ﬁeld with various neuronal subtypes and distinct differentially affected intra-neuronal compartments. Modelling of neurodegeneration requires faithful in vitro separation of axons and dendrites, their distal and proximal compartments as well as organelle tracking with deﬁned retrograde versus anterograde directionality. We use microﬂuidic chambers to achieve compartmentalization and established high throughput live organelle imaging at standardized distal and proximal axonal readout sites in iPSC-derived spinal motor neuron cultures from human amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patients to study trafﬁcking phenotypes of potential disease relevance. Our semi- automated pipeline of organelle tracking with FIJI and KNIME yields quantitative, multiparametric high content phenotypic signatures of organelle morphology and their trafﬁcking in axons. We provide here the resultant large datasets to enable systemic signature interrogations for comprehensive and predictive disease modelling, mechanistic dissection and secondary hit validation (e.g. drug screens, genetic screens).

describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text.quote:
While Z-scores served as measure for the degree and signiﬁcance of deviation from ctrl proximal
                               conditions for individual parameters, we wished to assess our multiparametric signatures as a whole with
                               respect to similarities and deviations. This has the advantage of discriminating between disease and
                               physiologic states more objectively, as comprehensive consideration of multiple parameters is less prone
                               to possible noise and bias of individual parameters. To this end, we performed unsupervised clustering of
                               the multiparametric data sets in KNIME. To avoid high dependencies on single parameters,
                               normalization of data is necessary prior to clustering, a requirement already fulﬁlled with the parameter
                               Z-score calculation above. Thus, we used the entire Z-score signature of all parameters above for
                               clustering in KNIME with the “Hierarchical clustering” node, which uses a bottom-up strategy. We used
                               the Euclidean average distance to agglomerate data and calculate the dendrograms (Figs 2d, 3c, 4d).
describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text.note: Extracted text evidence: While Z-scores served as measure for the degree and signiﬁcance of deviation from ctrl proximal conditions for individual parameters, we wished to assess our multiparametric signatures as a whole with respect to similarities and deviations. This has the advantage of discriminating between disease and physiologic states more objectively, as comprehensive consideration of multiple parameters is less prone to possible noise and bias of individual parameters. To this end, we performed unsupervised clustering of the multiparametric data sets in KNIME. To avoid high dependencies on single parameters, normalization of data is necessary prior to clustering, a requirement already fulﬁlled with the parameter Z-score calculation above. Thus, we used the entire Z-score signature of all parameters above for clustering in KNIME with the “Hierarchical clustering” node, which uses a bottom-up strategy. We used the Euclidean average distance to agglomerate data and calculate the dendrograms (Figs 2d, 3c, 4d).

description_audit_fields.description: Map of human-readable and traceability fields. These preserve identifiers, labels, simplified article-relation labels and other categorical values, version strings, and evidence notes.

flag_audit_fields.description: Map of statistics-ready presence flags for deeper workflow-reporting audit cases. A true value means the article assessment found the named item/category; a false value means that item/category was absent, not reported, not applicable, not assessed, or not verified in the current evidence. Records with knime_article_relation=about_knime keep this map empty because they are background/platform papers.

item_shape.field_name: true means presence; false means absence or no supporting evidence

flag_audit_fields.absence_and_uncertainty_storage: Absence, not-applicable, not-assessed, unclear, and not-checked classifications are preserved as strings in description_audit_fields rather than as true flags.
flag_audit_fields.knime_relation_note: Detailed KNIME presentation categories were replaced by description_audit_fields.knime_article_relation and the presence flag uses_knime.
flag_audit_fields.workflow_text_description_note: describes_workflow_or_nodes_in_text is true when nodes_or_components_reported contains named KNIME workflow steps, nodes, modules, or components recorded during assessment.

flag_audit_support.description: Map from true flag_audit_fields names to citation objects supporting the presence claim. Article-text support includes extracted_text_lines, quote, article_section, and note. Resource URLs and manual classifications are marked with source labels such as linked_resources, article_audit_fields, or manual_assessment.

flag_name.extracted_text_lines: extracted-text line range or source label
flag_name.quote: direct article wording when extracted_text_lines is a numeric line range
flag_name.article_section: article section containing the quoted wording when known

article_text_source.method: Raw text is generated from local PDFs with scripts/extract_article_texts.py using pdftotext -layout; processed reading files are generated with scripts/normalize_article_text_columns.py.
article_text_source.line_reference_semantics: extracted_text_lines refer to processed one-column text files under data/processed/articles, not original PDF page lines.
